diary:
Woke up way too late this morning, but honestly the instant ramen hit different before scrolling through Discord for like twenty minutes. Campus was already brutal by the time I made it to Allen—that 91-degree heat was no joke. Spent a solid chunk of the day cramming Data Structures problem sets in the library, which felt productive even if the AC was working overtime.

Lunch at the food court was just a cold pho situation because anything warm seemed insane. Data Structures lecture went fine, notes were decent. The real save was hitting up IMA for pickup basketball after—needed that air conditioning and a good sweat to reset my brain.

Gas Works Park was packed for the concert, but it was worth it. Sitting on the grass with people I barely knew, watching the sunset while the haze hung over everything... there was something peaceful about that, even if the smoke from BC was kind of a downer. Ended the night at Taneda with a bowl of warm ramen, which felt like the perfect full-circle moment after all that heat.
thought_process: I think I was chasing balance today without really planning it. The heat made me want to stay still, but my body needed movement, so basketball became this anchor point. I chose cold food early because the weather was physically demanding, but by evening I craved something warm and comforting—not just for nutrition but for the ritual of it. The concert wasn't something I'd normally prioritize on a school night, but the outdoor vibe and crowd energy felt restorative after hours inside studying. The smoke from BC wildfires created this hazy, almost melancholic backdrop that made me more introspective than usual. My mood shifted from groggy-to-focused during study hours, then energized at IMA, then contemplative at Gas Works. The day felt less like a checklist and more like riding different waves of what I needed.
world_event_prompt: Hot, clear Wednesday hitting 91F by mid-afternoon. Free outdoor concert at Gas Works Park from 6pm draws big crowds. Moderate air quality from distant BC wildfire smoke.
